etimes500-year-old Toronto seeds defy death! Ancient seeds sprout after a century undergroundDuring Toronto's Port Lands construction, ancient seeds, some dating back to the 1500s, were unearthed from a buried marsh. These viable seeds, preserved by waterlogged conditions, are now sprouting, offering a glimpse into the region's past biodiversity. This discovery aids in understanding and restoring the lost Ashbridge's Marsh.
